name: Chicken and Lentils in Clay
recipeIngredient: 1 large Garlic clove, minced or pressed
1 medium Onion, thinly sliced, separated into rings
1/2 cup Slivered smoked pork shoulder or ham
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F. Rinse chicken; pat dry. Coarsely chop liver and reserve. (Discard remaining giblets, or save them for another use.) Line a 3-quart unglazed clay cooker with parchment paper, trimming the paper even with the rim of the baking dish. Place chicken, breast up, within the parchment.
2. Place lentils around the chicken. Combine garlic, onion, celery, ham, carrot, parsley, and chicken liver; place mixture and bay leaf atop lentils. Sprinkle with salt and pepper. Heat together beef broth, wine, and mustard; pour over chicken.
3. Cover and bake, stirring lentils occasionally, until chicken is browned and lentils are tender (about 3 hours). Carve chicken and serve with lentils.
